metadata.summary: OpenAI API with Chat Completions, Responses API, Structured Outputs, Batch API (50% discount). Pin exact model versions, use Structured Outputs, set max_tokens.

content.title: OpenAI API Integration
content.summary: OpenAI Platform provides Chat Completions, the Responses API (2025+, stateful), Structured Outputs, Batch API (50% discount), embeddings, Whisper, TTS, DALL-E, and fine-tuning. Pin exact model versions (gpt-4o-2024-08-06). Use Structured Outputs (beta.chat.completions.parse) not JSON mode. Set max_tokens on every call. For new projects, prefer the Responses API over Chat Completions — it manages conversation state server-side.

why.p: The OpenAI API is the most widely supported LLM integration target, with the strongest ecosystem for structured output (native schema enforcement), function calling, and the Batch API for 50% cost reduction on non-time-sensitive workloads. Pinning model versions prevents silent behavior changes; exponential backoff handles rate limits gracefully.

list.item: Production app requiring best-in-class structured output with native schema enforcement
Vision tasks requiring GPT-4o's image understanding
Batch processing of non-time-sensitive data (50% cost discount via Batch API)
Function calling / tool use as the primary agentic mechanism
Audio transcription (Whisper) or TTS in agent pipelines
Responses API's built-in conversation state management is needed
Privacy-sensitive data that must not leave your infrastructure (use local Ollama instead)
Very long context (>128K tokens) — use Claude (200K) or Gemini (2M)
When Anthropic Claude's instruction-following is more reliable for your specific task
Tight budget with high-volume simple tasks — Gemini Flash ($0.10/M) is cheaper than gpt-4o-mini ($0.15/M)
When you need >128K context for a single call (use gpt-4.1 with 1M context or alternatives)
Chat Completions (/v1/chat/completions): text generation, conversation, vision, function calling
Responses API (/v1/responses): stateful API (2025+); manages conversation history server-side via previous_response_id
Embeddings (/v1/embeddings): text-embedding-3-large for RAG, semantic search
Batch (/v1/batches): 50% cost discount, 24h completion window, JSONL input
Audio (/v1/audio/transcriptions, /v1/audio/speech): Whisper for STT, TTS models
Images (/v1/images/generations): DALL-E 3 for image generation

rule.statement: Default model: gpt-4o for quality-sensitive tasks; gpt-4o-mini for classification, routing, and formatting (90% cheaper). Use o3-mini for complex reasoning. Always pin exact version: `gpt-4o-2024-08-06` not `gpt-4o`.
rule.rationale: Non-pinned model aliases change behavior on OpenAI's update schedule without warning. A silent model upgrade can break prompt-sensitive production systems.

rule.statement: For new projects, use the Responses API over Chat Completions. Use `previous_response_id` for multi-turn conversations — it eliminates manual history management and reduces payload size.
rule.rationale: Chat Completions requires the developer to send the full conversation history on every call. The Responses API manages history server-side, reducing payload size and developer surface area. OpenAI is actively developing the Responses API; Chat Completions is in maintenance mode.

rule.statement: Use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse(response_format=MyModel)` instead of JSON mode. Structured Outputs guarantee schema compliance; JSON mode only guarantees valid JSON syntax.
rule.rationale: JSON mode failures are silent: valid JSON with missing or wrong-typed fields passes JSON parsing but fails downstream. Native Structured Outputs enforce the schema at the model level.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel from openai import OpenAI
class Response(BaseModel): answer: str confidence: float
client = OpenAI() result = client.beta.chat.completions.parse( model="gpt-4o-2024-08-06", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "..."}], response_format=Response, ) typed_result: Response = result.choices[0].message.parsed

rule.statement: Set `max_tokens` on every API call. Unbounded output generation runs up costs and stalls agents waiting for a response that never completes.
rule.rationale: Without max_tokens, a stuck generation can produce thousands of tokens. In agentic loops, this blocks all downstream steps. `finish_reason: "length"` signals truncation — check for it.

rule.statement: Implement exponential backoff for 429 (rate limit) and 5xx errors: start at 1s, cap at 60s, max 6 retries. Use `time.sleep(min(2**attempt, 60))` pattern.
rule.rationale: Rate limits are transient. Immediate retry hammers the quota further. Exponential backoff distributes retries over time and recovers from temporary limits gracefully.

rule.statement: Use service account keys (`sk-svcacct-*`) for automated systems — never user keys. Keep all API keys server-side; never expose them in client code.
rule.rationale: User keys have access to all projects in an account. Service account keys are scoped to a single project, limiting blast radius if compromised.

rule.statement: Enable prompt caching by placing static system prompts first and never modifying their prefix. Track cache hits via `response.usage` to verify caching is active.
rule.rationale: OpenAI's prompt cache activates on prefix matches. Even a single character change in a cached prefix invalidates it. Static system prompts placed first (before any dynamic content) maximize cache hit rate.

list.item: Rate limits are hard walls: tier-based (3–10K RPM); no graceful degradation by default
Model versions are periodically deprecated without warning — pin exact versions in production (`gpt-4o-2024-08-06`)
JSON mode does NOT guarantee schema compliance — use Structured Outputs (`response_format=MyModel`) instead
Streaming + structured output: `beta.chat.completions.parse` buffers internally; latency gain is minimal
Context length limit (128K for gpt-4o) is strict — exceeded requests fail with 400, not a graceful fallback
Costs scale unpredictably with agentic loops that generate long outputs; always set `max_tokens`
Responses API (2025+) does not support all parameters available in Chat Completions; check docs before migrating

antipattern.description: Tool call response missing `tool_call_id` matching the request.
antipattern.reason: OpenAI requires each tool result to reference the exact `tool_call_id` from the request. Mismatches cause HTTP 400 errors. Always map results back to the call ID.

antipattern.description: Mixing Responses API and Chat Completions in the same conversation flow.
antipattern.reason: The two APIs manage conversation history differently. Responses API stores history server-side; Chat Completions requires developer-managed history. Mixing them produces inconsistent context.

antipattern.description: Submitting a Batch API job and then waiting synchronously for results.
antipattern.reason: Batch API results arrive asynchronously (up to 24h). Synchronous waiting will time out. Use a separate completion-handler agent or poll the batch status endpoint.

antipattern.description: Using `finish_reason: "length"` response downstream without checking truncation.
antipattern.reason: A truncated response means the model was cut off mid-output. Passing incomplete data downstream silently corrupts all dependent states. Always check finish_reason.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agents use OpenAI via function calling (tools array) for structured decisions and the Batch API for high-volume background processing. The Responses API (`/v1/responses`) simplifies stateful multi-turn conversations by managing history server-side via `previous_response_id`, reducing the payload size agents must pass around. For tool-using agents, define tools as Pydantic models converted to JSON Schema; the model will call them deterministically. Always handle `tool_calls` in agent loops with explicit retry on parsing failures.

list.item: `/faion` (knowledge/solo/sdd/sdd/) — uses OpenAI structured output for quality gate decisions
Any custom extraction agent using `client.beta.chat.completions.parse`
Pin exact model versions: `gpt-4o-2024-08-06` not `gpt-4o` — snapshot versions don't change behavior
Use Structured Outputs (`beta.chat.completions.parse`) instead of JSON mode for schema compliance
Set `max_tokens` on every call — unbounded output generation runs up costs and stalls agents
Implement exponential backoff for 429s: start at 1s, cap at 60s, max 6 retries
Use `usage` from response to track input/output token spend per agent step
Enable prompt caching by placing static system prompts first and never modifying their prefix
Use `gpt-4o-mini` for routing/classification steps, `gpt-4o` only for complex reasoning
Use service account keys (`sk-svcacct-*`) for automated systems, not user keys

section.name: CLI Tools
section.p: `openai` (Python SDK): All API endpoints, streaming, structured output. `pip install openai` / github.com/openai/openai-python
`openai` (Node SDK): TypeScript/JS applications. `npm install openai` / github.com/openai/openai-node
`tiktoken`: Token counting for budget/context management. `pip install tiktoken` / github.com/openai/tiktoken
`instructor`: Structured output with retry across providers. `pip install instructor` / python.useinstructor.com
OpenAI CLI (`openai`): Upload files, manage fine-tunes, batches. installed with SDK

section.name: Services and Apps
section.p: OpenAI Platform (SaaS): Primary; usage dashboard, rate limit self-service
OpenAI Batch API (SaaS): 50% discount, 24h window; ideal for bulk agent tasks
LangSmith (SaaS): Full OpenAI call tracing; prompt versioning
Langfuse (OSS): Self-hosted tracing for OpenAI calls via OTEL
Helicone (SaaS proxy): 1-line integration; caches, rate-limits, logs OpenAI calls
Portkey (SaaS): Multi-provider fallback: OpenAI → Anthropic → Gemini

template.purpose: Exponential backoff wrapper for rate limit and server errors
Function-calling agent loop with tool execution
Batch API JSONL builder and job submission
